one_line_thesis: ADP is the dominant US payroll/HCM processor with sticky recurring revenue and strong cash conversion; at ~$255 the filing-based synthesis implies ~12% per year, below our accumulate hurdle — watch pending client-fund spread normalization.

deep_dive.executive_summary: ADP is a payroll and HCM compounder with sticky recurring revenue, strong cash conversion, and heavy capital return ($3.7 billion returned in FY2025 via dividends and buybacks). FY2025 free cash flow per share is about $11.67; at ~$255 (July 2026), total synthesis implies 12.0% per year over 7 years on filing-based owner cash. Moat is stable (switching costs, compliance scale) but not wide; partial dhando because net debt (~$13 per share) and client-fund rate sensitivity cap the bear case. Marvin proposes watch on math (below ~15% accumulate hurdle); [HUMAN REVIEW] whether FY2025 client-fun...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: stockholders' equity before client funds ~$6.2B; economic value in normalized owner cash ($11.6736/sh), not GAAP book alone.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of ADP, including payroll/HCM owner cash, PEO platform upside, net financial claims, and competition/client-funds reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Consolidated payroll engine valued through owner-cash discount on FY2025 FCF per share; PEO option, net liquidity, and competition reserve are separate overlap keys.

risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: PEO pass-through is client-funded; incremental capex minimal.

timeline.label: Q2 FY2026: service revenue $6.50B (+6% YoY); diluted EPS $5.12 H1 (+9%); OCF $1.78B Q2 — payroll retention and PEO mix shift intact
FY2025 results: service revenue $20.56B (+7%); net income $4.08B; OCF $4.94B; $3.68B returned via dividends and buybacks
FY2025 capital return: dividends $2.40B and share repurchases $1.28B; diluted share count down to 408.7M
Shelf registration (S-3ASR) filed for ongoing debt and equity flexibility
Maria Black became president and CEO, succeeding Carlos Rodriguez after long tenure
Accelerated PEO and HCM platform investments; co-employment model expanded as recurring revenue pillar
Automatic Payrolls, Inc. incorporated; evolved into global payroll and HCM franchise listed as ADP on NYSE

industry.structure: Duopoly-plus in US payroll processing: ADP and Paychex dominate mid-market and enterprise; Workday and UKG compete on cloud HCM. High switching costs on tax compliance and employee records create sticky recurring revenue.
industry.share_shift: ADP maintains scale leadership globally; PEO co-employment growing faster than core payroll; cloud-native competitors gain share in new-logo HCM but struggle to displace installed payroll base.
industry.trend: Interest rates affect spreads on client-fund balances (material to Employer Services economics); wage inflation supports revenue per worksite employee; regulatory complexity (tax, benefits, ACA) reinforces outsourcing.

top_claims.claim: ADP is a payroll and HCM compounder with sticky recurring revenue, strong cash conversion, and heavy capital return ($3.7 billion returned in FY2025 via dividends and buybacks). FY2025 free cash flow per share is about $11.67; at ~$255 (July 2026), total synthesis implies 12.0% per year over 7 years on filing-based own
*Primary risk: Client-fund interest revenue compresses faster than payroll fee growth if rates fall, reducing high-margin spread income without offsetting cost cuts.
Competition: Paychex price pressure in mid-market; Workday/UKG win new HCM logos.
Leverage: ~$8.7B total debt (commercial paper + long-term) against $3.3B cash; refinancing risk in stress.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Client-fund interest revenue compresses faster than payroll fee growth if rates fall, reducing high-margin spread income without offsetting cost cuts.
Competition: Paychex price pressure in mid-market; Workday/UKG win new HCM logos.
